title: Newborn feeding review

examination.text: Requires maternal physical and emotional assessment, postpartum haemorrhage and hypertensive-risk screening, feeding support, family planning discussion, continuity, and EMR documentation.
examination.rationale: Supports feeding support and continuity documentation for newborn feeding review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Requires maternal physical and emotional assessment, postpartum haemorrhage and hypertensive-risk screening, feeding support, family planning discussion, continuity, and EMR documentation.

exact_section.heading: 3.1 Core Components — maternal physical and emotional health, education, support, and continuity

examination.text: Specifies vital signs, uterine involution, lochia, wound healing, bladder and bowel function, feeding, mood, support, family planning, pelvic floor, indicated tests, and documented follow-up.
examination.rationale: Supports postnatal feeding and documented follow-up context for newborn feeding review; no decision is generated.
examination.evidence_extract: Specifies vital signs, uterine involution, lochia, wound healing, bladder and bowel function, feeding, mood, support, family planning, pelvic floor, indicated tests, and documented follow-up.

exact_section.heading: Appendices 2 and 3 — detailed maternal postnatal and home-visit checks

follow_up.text: Defines Abu Dhabi postnatal services and four checks through six weeks, with timing and mode adjusted for risk.
follow_up.rationale: Supports postnatal services for newborns through six weeks for newborn feeding review; no decision is generated.
follow_up.evidence_extract: Defines Abu Dhabi postnatal services and four checks through six weeks, with timing and mode adjusted for risk.

source_limitations: All item-level mappings remain unsupported pending separate clinician review and signed approval.
Age and gestation, feeding method and frequency, latch, transfer, intake, output, weight, hydration, jaundice, examination and clinician plan are absent.
No diagnosis, interpretation, investigation, treatment, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, escalation or disposition is generated for newborn feeding review.
Any legacy item not explicitly mapped to an exact section remains unsupported pending qualified clinician review.
No mapping authorises an automatic diagnosis, differential, investigation, management decision, medicine, dose, procedure, referral, urgency conclusion, discharge decision, or patient instruction.
curation_policy: Direct guideline curation: retain only explicitly mapped content; rewrite only with explicit item mapping; remove unmapped legacy content; add only committed exact-section evidence summaries.
